input: Sentence: Transport Layer • Takes data from higher levels of OSI Model and breaks it into segments that can be sent to lower-level layers for data transmission • Conversely, reassembles data segments into data that higher-level protocols and applications can use • Also puts segments in correct order (called sequencing ) so they can be reassembled in correct order at destination • Concerned with the reliability of the transport of sent data • May use a connection-oriented protocol such as TCP to ensure destination received segments • May use a connectionless protocol such as UDP to send segments without assurance of delivery • Uses port addressing Entity1: Reliability (CONCEPT). instruction: Identify the relation between the two entities using ONLY these labels: used_for, based_on, implements, part_of, improves, no_relation.
input: Sentence: Data Link Layer • Is responsible for moving frames from node to node or computer to computer • Can move frames from one adjacent computer to another, cannot move frames across routers • Encapsulation = frame • Requires MAC address or physical address • Protocols defined include Ethernet Protocol and Point-to-Point Protocol (PPP) • Device example: Switch • Two sublayers: Logical Link Control (LLC) and the Media Access Control (MAC) o Logical Link Control (LLC) – Data Link layer addressing, flow control, address notification, error control o Media Access Control (MAC) –Determines which computer has access to the network media at any given time –Determines where one frame ends and the next one starts, called frame synchronization Entity1: Data Link Layer (TOPIC). Entity2: Protocol (CONCEPT).

instruction: Identify the relation between the two entities using ONLY these labels: used_for, based_on, implements, part_of, improves, no_relation.
input: Sentence: Transport Layer • Takes data from higher levels of OSI Model and breaks it into segments that can be sent to lower-level layers for data transmission • Conversely, reassembles data segments into data that higher-level protocols and applications can use • Also puts segments in correct order (called sequencing ) so they can be reassembled in correct order at destination • Concerned with the reliability of the transport of sent data • May use a connection-oriented protocol such as TCP to ensure destination received segments • May use a connectionless protocol such as UDP to send segments without assurance of delivery • Uses port addressing Entity1: OSI Model (TOPIC). Entity2: Transport Layer (TOPIC).

Entity2: Protocol (CONCEPT).

input: Sentence: File operations are standardized system calls enabling core file management: create allocates new space and adds a directory entry after checking availability; open searches the directory, loads the FCB into the open-file table, and returns a descriptor for quick access; read fetches data from the current file pointer position (with separate read/write pointers for efficiency); write appends or overwrites at the pointer, often updating metadata; close flushes changes and removes the entry from open-file tables; seek (reposition) adjusts the pointer without data transfer; delete frees space and erases the directory entry; truncate shortens the file while preserving attributes; and append adds to the end Entity1: System Calls (TOPIC).
